spike: Phase 9 — knowledge injection (arm 1: LoRA SFT via MLX)
hardware: Apple M4, 16GB; MLX-LoRA fp16, batch_size 2, max_seq 1024; peak mem 3.5-5.3GB, no reboot

dataset.source: examples/kg-telegram-sink graph (738 ents / 1119 rels) via Phase-7 toKbTriples
dataset.split: by entity (eval entities unseen in training)

runs.name: aggressive
runs.note: injects strongest but CATASTROPHIC forgetting (ppl 200x base)

runs.name: gentle
runs.note: general LM preserved (ppl 1.8x base), modest recall

runs.name: balanced
runs.note: best operating point — recall 27x base, ppl 3.9x base (functional)

decision.injection: GO — wanshi triples inject into Qwen3-0.6B via MLX-LoRA; recall 27x base at a usable point
decision.stability: GO with care — forgetting is real & config-sensitive; light/balanced configs keep the model functional (recall<->forgetting tradeoff)
decision.refusal: NO for plain LoRA — 0% refusal across all configs (positive-only SFT). This is the decisive gap and the case for arm 2 (KBLaM native refusal / CURLoRA + refusal-negatives)
